title: The uncrossing partial order on matchings is Eulerian
why_relevant: Directly studies matchings of 2n boundary points ordered by resolving crossings. Its Eulerian and rank arguments are central for understanding any CW complex whose cells are built from crossing matchings and uncrossing maps.

title: Shellability of face posets of electrical networks and the CW poset property
why_relevant: Proves shellability and the CW-poset property for uncrossing posets of matchings. This is one of the closest available topological results to the complex F_w, especially if F_w can be realized as an interval, subposet, or labeled subcomplex of an uncrossing poset.

title: The cd-Indices of Intervals in the Uncrossing Partial Order on Matchings
why_relevant: Analyzes intervals in the uncrossing order and gives recursive and order-reversing descriptions. Interval structure is likely relevant because cells of F_w are attached by resolving subsets of crossings.

title: Circular Planar Electrical Networks I: The Electrical Poset EP_n
why_relevant: Introduces and studies the electrical poset, one origin of the uncrossing order on medial matchings. It supplies combinatorial and topological descriptions of crossing-resolution posets that may model the unlabeled version of F_w.

title: Electroid varieties and a compactification of the space of electrical networks
why_relevant: Relates matchings, uncrossing order, noncrossing partitions, affine Bruhat order, and a compactified electrical-network space. This gives a geometric route for proving contractibility or ball-like behavior of crossing-resolution complexes.

title: The space of circular planar electrical networks
why_relevant: Develops coordinates and cell-structure compatibility for circular planar electrical networks. The degeneration of conductances to boundary cells parallels resolving crossings in a matching complex.

title: The totally nonnegative Grassmannian is a ball
why_relevant: Proves that Lam's compactification of the space of electrical networks is homeomorphic to a closed ball. This is highly relevant if F_w can be embedded into or compared with the electrical-network compactification or its face stratification.

title: Regularity theorem for totally nonnegative flag varieties
why_relevant: Proves regular CW and ball-closure results for totally nonnegative cell decompositions. Its techniques may help show that crossing-resolution cell complexes have contractible cell closures or regular CW structures.

title: Generalized recursive atom ordering and equivalence to CL-shellability
why_relevant: Develops shellability tools and applies them to uncrossing posets. These methods are natural candidates for proving contractibility of labeled subcomplexes via recursive atom orderings or CL-shellings.

title: CW posets after the Poincare conjecture
why_relevant: Gives criteria for posets to be face posets of regular CW complexes, especially using Eulerian and shellability hypotheses. This is useful for converting combinatorial information about crossing-resolution posets into topological conclusions.

title: Subword complexes in Coxeter groups
why_relevant: Subword complexes are shellable balls or spheres built from choices of resolving/deleting letters in words. Since uncrossing posets are related to Bruhat order, these results may provide an alternative shellability or contractibility framework for F_w.

title: On triangulations of surfaces
why_relevant: Contains fundamental contractibility arguments for arc and triangulation complexes of surfaces. Noncrossing matchings of boundary points are arc systems in a disk, so Hatcher-style surgery and link arguments are likely useful.

title: A User's Guide to Discrete Morse Theory
why_relevant: Discrete Morse theory is a standard method for proving finite CW or cubical complexes contractible by constructing an acyclic matching. It is a natural tool for collapsing F_w by reducing crossings or choosing canonical cancellations.

title: Valid plane trees: Combinatorial models for RNA secondary structures with Watson-Crick base pairs
why_relevant: Introduces foldings of complementary words around plane trees, equivalently noncrossing perfect matchings of inverse/complementary letters. This is directly relevant to the vertices of F_w and to greedy or inductive constructions of valid matchings.

title: k-Foldability of Words
why_relevant: Studies words over complementary alphabets and the number of noncrossing perfect matchings/foldings. Its characterizations and greedy folding ideas may help analyze the set of 0-cells of F_w for a fixed reducible word.

title: The cancellation norm and the geometry of bi-invariant word metrics
why_relevant: Develops foldings and cancellation pairings for words in free groups, including algorithms based on matching inverse letters. This is useful for translating reducible words into structured cancellation diagrams.

title: Crossings and Nestings of Matchings and Partitions
why_relevant: Provides detailed combinatorics of crossing and nesting statistics for matchings. These techniques can help organize induction on crossing number and understand how resolving crossings changes matching structure.

title: Diagram groups and directed 2-complexes: homotopy and homology
why_relevant: Develops Squier complexes and proves contractibility results for their universal covers. F_w resembles a cubical rewriting/resolution complex for free reductions, so diagram-group techniques are a plausible route.

title: An introduction to diagram groups
why_relevant: Surveys Squier complexes, diagram groups, CAT(0) cube complexes, and hyperplanes. It is useful for identifying whether F_w is a Squier-type cube complex or can be studied by diagram-group methods.

title: Hyperplanes of Squier's cube complexes
why_relevant: Analyzes hyperplanes and decompositions of Squier cube complexes. If F_w admits a cubical or median structure, these hyperplane methods could help prove nonpositive curvature or contractibility.

title: Groupoids and the algebra of rewriting in group presentations
why_relevant: Builds Squier-type complexes for group presentations and rewriting systems. This is relevant because the word w is reducible in a free group, and F_w may be interpretable as a space of derivations between reductions.

title: Stallings foldings and the subgroup structure of free groups
why_relevant: Gives a detailed combinatorial treatment of folding methods in free groups. Although graph foldings differ from boundary matchings, the paper provides standard free-group folding and cancellation techniques that may inform a proof.
